symptom: drone calls shell_exec mid-WRITE phase to 'verify the build'; schema filter rejects because shell_exec isn't in the scaffold-first/pre-first-write allowlist; worse, phase_machine's compile-nudge tells the drone to run shell_exec anyway
root_cause: two coupled defects — (1) phase_machine emits '[PHASE:WRITE] Time to compile. Run: shell_exec with npx vite build' unconditionally for any WRITE phase, but scaffold-first gamedev has no build step; (2) the dispatch reject error dumps sorted(allowed_names) without naming the right next action
fix_approach: (1) phase_machine.context_note: fire 'call message_result to deliver' instead of 'run shell_exec' for scaffold-first gamedev at iters_in_phase≥6 with files_written≥1; (2) agent.py dispatch reject: specialized helper for shell_exec — scaffold-first gamedev → 'no build step, call message_result'; plain WRITE → 'build runs automatically after file_write'
principle: v5 — the system's advice must align with the system's gates (a nudge that contradicts the schema is worse than no nudge)

desc: scaffold-first gamedev, 1 file written, 6 iters → deliver nudge (NOT compile nudge)
non-gamedev WRITE phase with code written → compile nudge preserved
legacy gamedev (no data dir) → compile nudge preserved — Round D path
under iters threshold → no compile nudge
